summary: Diff implements hardening around browser history.state usage in useExperienceNavigationState and adds expandable technical error details to the ErrorBoundary UI, plus additional diagnostic context logging. One potential gap is that the landing page may still show a blocking loading spinner until initialization completes, which could conflict with the requirement that the landing page reliably renders on first load.

missing_requirements.requirement: Opening the app in a fresh tab renders the landing page instead of the ErrorBoundary fallback; hard-refresh does not crash on initial render; fix prevents runtime exceptions during initial navigation/history state initialization.
missing_requirements.reason: The navigation/history initialization appears hardened (supporting the intent of REQ-1), but MindVaultLandingPage introduces a blocking loading screen when !isInitialized, which may prevent the landing page from rendering immediately on first load (depending on timing). The diff does not show a change that guarantees the landing content renders regardless of initialization.
evidence: frontend/src/hooks/useExperienceNavigationState.ts (sets isInitialized true only after effect runs)

hallucinated_features.description: Added a blocking loading spinner screen (“Loading...”) on the landing page while navigation state initialization completes.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest focuses on preventing an initial-load crash and hardening history-state syncing; it does not request adding a new loading UI state, and this could change first-load behavior.

hallucinated_features.description: Introduced additional navigation-context diagnostics plumbing (setExperienceIntendedOpen + logNavigationContext) beyond the UI requirement for showing error details.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-3 asks to surface runtime error details in the UI; adding separate navigation-context logging/flags is not explicitly requested (though it is related diagnostics).
